sample_tweets.text: 2 / 2  이 시점, 즉 2018년 중반은 대한민국에서도 주목해야 할 해다. 같은 해 NIST에 CVE-2018-10561·10562가 등재되었고, 문재인 정부의 블록체인 온라인 투표 로드맵이 수립되었으며, 핸디소프트가 전자투표용 블록체인 개발에 착수한 것으로 보도된 해다. 한미 양국에서 2018년은 선거 인프라를 둘러싼 적대 세력의 움직임과 이를 향한 제도적 변화가 동시에 집중된 시점이었다. 요컨대, 선거 인프라를 지배하려는 거대한 구조적 전환기가 한미 양국에서 동시에 시작되었던 것이다.  대한민국 사전투표 시스템의 근간은 2013년 구축된 중앙선관위 서버의 통합선거인명부다. 전국 시·군·구별로 분산 관리되던 선거인 명부가 중앙 서버로 통합된 이 시스템은 어느 투표소에서든 실시간으로 선거인 자격을 확인하고 투표용지를 즉석 발급할 수 있게 해준다.   그러나 바로 이 중앙 집중화가 가장 치명적인 취약점이기도 하다. 미국 정보당국이 '중앙 집중화된 선거 관련 데이터 저장소가 가장 취약하다'고 평가한 논리는 대한민국 통합선거인명부에 그대로 적용된다. 단 하나의 서버를 장악하면 전국의 사전투표 전체가 동시에 통제 가능해지기 때문이다.   그리고 트럼프 대통령이 미국 선거 인프라 침해 역량을 보유한 세력으로 지목한 북한 정찰총국은, 하나프로그람센터를 통해 10년 이상에 걸쳐 바로 이 통합선거인명부와 연결된 통신망의 핵심 기술 구조를 이해하고 있는 유일한 외부 행위자다. 이 지점이 단둥 프로젝트가 강조하는 구조적 위협의 본질이다.  트럼프 대통령의 이 연설은 단둥 프로젝트가 구성한 위협 구조가 대한민국만의 문제가 아님을 미국 행정부 최고위급에서 공식 확인한 최초의 사례다.   중국 공산당의 선거 데이터 수집, 북한 정찰총국의 선거 인프라 침해 역량, 취약한 전자 선거 인프라, 유권자 명부의 보안 결함은 한미 양국이 공유하는 문제다. 그리고 그 취약점의 기술적 뿌리를 단둥의 하나프로그람센터로 추적하는 것이 핵심 주장이다. 통합선거인명부는 이 모든 것의 교차점이다.   내 외부에서 침투 가능한 통신망, 취약점이 공식 확인된 장비, 그리고 모든 선거인 데이터가 집중된 단일 중앙 서버—이 세 요소가 결합된 구조에서 통합선거인명부는 선거 조작의 가장 효율적인 단일 접점이 된다. 트럼프 대통령이 지목한 북한이 그 접점의 기술적 설계에 깊이 관여하였다는 것이 단둥 프로젝트가 제시하는 가장 심각한 결론이다.  이로써 트럼프는 선거 제도의 봉인을 풀고, 거대한 부정선거 투쟁의 서막을 세계 만천하에 고했다.

𝐆𝐨𝐥𝐝𝐞𝐧👁️ - 𝐅𝐢𝐛𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐜𝐜𝐢 𝐑𝐞𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬: 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐁𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐓𝐨𝐨𝐥𝐬 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐁𝐮𝐲𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐮𝐥𝐥𝐛𝐚𝐜𝐤𝐬  The Fibonacci Retracement tool helps traders measure how deep a pullback is within an existing trend. It isn't a buy signal by itself, it's a framework for identifying high-probability support zones where price may reverse.  ➡️ 23.6%–38.2% retracement: Very shallow pullback. Buyers are aggressive and the trend is strong. Strong trends often don't retrace much before making new highs.  ➡️ 50% retracement: A healthy, balanced pullback. Bulls are taking profits while buyers begin stepping back in.  ➡️ 61.8% - 78.6% (OTE Zone): This is where many of the best risk-to-reward entries develop. A deeper pullback shakes out weak hands, allowing stronger buyers to accumulate before the next leg higher, if the uptrend remains intact.  ➡️ Key Tips:  •Never trade Fibonacci levels in isolation. Wait for confirmation such as a bullish candlestick pattern, increasing volume, or momentum indicators turning higher.  •Always draw Fibonacci from the swing low to the swing high in an uptrend (reverse it in a downtrend).  •Combine Fibonacci with horizontal support, moving averages, trendlines, or volume profile to build confluence.  •Use the recent swing low as your invalidation level to define risk before entering.  •Remember: Fibonacci highlights areas of interest, not guaranteed reversal points.  The best traders don't buy every pullback, they wait for price to pull back into multiple levels of confluence, confirm the reversal, and then let probability work in their favor. #threatreport #LowCompleteness Inside Russia’s Camera-Hacking Espionage Campaign | 16-07-2026 Source: censys.com/blog/russia-camer… Key details below ↓  💀Threats: Dropbear_tool,  🎯Victims: Internet connected cameras, Manufacturing, Energy, Banking  🏭Industry: Financial, Transport, Military, Energy  🌐Geo: Russian, Poland, Greece, Belgium, Turkey, Austria, Spain, Croatia, Russia, Luxembourg, Ireland, North macedonia, Finland, Denmark, Hungary, Albania, Slovakia, Sweden, Ukraine, Latvia, Romania, Slovenia, Lithuania, Estonia, Portugal, United kingdom, Italy, France, Netherlands, Cyprus, Malta, Norway, Germany, Montenegro, Iceland, Czechia, Bulgaria  🔓CVEs: CVE-2016-7407  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2016-740…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *9.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: Unknown     Soft:  - dropbear_ssh_project dropbear_ssh (le2016.73)  CVE-2021-39275  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2021-392…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *9.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: Unknown     Soft:  - apache http_server (<2.4.49)  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1190, T1591.003  🔠Functions: Internet-Connected  🗂️Win API: ARC  #threatreport: Recent analysis has highlighted the extensive vulnerability present in Internet-connected cameras, notably exploited by a Russian state actor for espionage. A study utilizing Censys data revealed that in the Netherlands, over 45,000 Internet-accessible cameras exist, with a significant number possessing known vulnerabilities. Specifically, 159 cameras are at risk from CVE-2016-7407, a long-standing arbitrary code execution flaw in the Dropbear SSH service, while 112 cameras could be compromised through CVE-2021-39275, a buffer overflow vulnerability in the Apache HTTP Server.  The situation is not isolated to the Netherlands, as analysis shows a troubling trend across Europe. More than 87,000 exploitable Internet-connected cameras were identified across various EU member states, NATO countries, and Ukraine, which includes over 4,000 cameras identified in Ukraine alone. It’s essential to note that these figures likely represent a conservative estimate, as scanning techniques may not capture every vulnerable device.  The exploitation of these cameras by state-aligned actors underscores broader challenges surrounding the security of Internet-connected devices. Many of these cameras do not house sensitive data; however, they serve as critical surveillance tools that can offer insights into operational patterns. For instance, unpatched cameras positioned at locations such as manufacturing facilities, energy utility substations, or banks can leak valuable information like shift schedules, logistical operations, and security measures. This passive reconnaissance does not necessitate breaching corporate networks; instead, it can stem from overlooking single, outdated devices.  Analyzing and mitigating these vulnerabilities is crucial for cybersecurity professionals. By utilizing platforms like Censys, CTI teams are able to monitor for devices vulnerable to CVE-2016-7407 and potentially other known threats within their jurisdictions. The capability to track and manage these vulnerabilities is essential in protecting the physical and digital security landscapes from state actors and other threat entities exploiting the weaknesses of Internet-connected devices.

Linuxカーネルのepollに、解放後利用（UAF）の脆弱性「Bad-Epoll」（CVE-2026-46242）が報告されています。epollは多数の接続や入出力の状態変化をまとめて監視する中核機能。2つの処理が同時に走ったときだけ成立する競合で、解放済みのメモリを使ってしまうというもの。成立する一瞬は約6命令分しかないとされますが、一般利用者から最高権限のrootを奪う実証コードが99%の成功率に達したとの報告。  epollには止めて回避する逃げ道がなく、対処はパッチ適用のみ、Linuxの権限昇格バグの多くはAndroidには効きませんが、本件は数少ない例外の一つとのこと。Webページの描画部分を閉じ込めておくChromeのサンドボックスの中からも成立するとされています。本家Linux（mainline）では修正済みですが、配布元のカーネルは取り込み状況が異なるため、版数だけでは自分の環境が直っているか判断できない点に注意が要ります。  【要点の整理】  ・epollの2つの後処理（close）が同時に走って衝突し、片方が解放したオブジェクトへもう片方が書き込むUAF。そこで得た8バイトの書き込みを足がかりに、解放された領域を別の構造体で埋め直すクロスキャッシュへ。さらに/proc/self/fdinfo経由でカーネルメモリを自由に読める状態へ持ち込み、最後は既存のコード片をつなぐROPでrootシェルに至るという流れ。カーネルを落とさずに再試行を繰り返す実装との説明。  ・公開PoCの対象は、Googleのカーネル脆弱性実証プログラムkernelCTFの長期サポート版6.12.67（99%）とCOS 121-18867.294.100（98%）。Androidはカーネル6.6以降のPixel 10でUAFを実際に引き起こすところまでで、root奪取までの完全な実証は開発中との説明。混入前の6.1系を使うPixel 8などは対象外との整理。  ・2023年4月8日のコミットでv6.4以降に混入し、2026年4月24日にmainlineで修正、5月30日にCVE公開。メンテナー側の最初のパッチでは直りきらず、正しい修正が入ったのは報告からおよそ2か月後という経緯。  ・この2件の競合は同じ1つのコミットで同時に入り込んだもの。片方のCVE-2026-43074はAnthropicのAI「Mythos」が発見した一方、Bad-Epollは取り逃したとの報告。競合の窓が狭く処理の割り込み順序を想像しにくいことに加え、43074が修正されると本件のUAFがKASAN（カーネルのメモリエラー検出機構）に引っかからず、動かしても異常が表に出にくいことが理由として挙げられている。  kernelCTFで実証された約130件のうちAndroidのroot取得候補は約10件とされ、本件もその一つに数えられています。狭いタイミングと乏しい痕跡が重なる競合状態のバグは、自動化された探索でも見つけにくい類いだと整理。報告時点で実際の悪用は確認されていないとのこと。  詳細は以下を参照： 技術開示： openwall.com/lists/oss-secur… kernelCTF向け公開PoC： github.com/J-jaeyoung/securi… CVEレコード： cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-202…  (※可能な範囲でファクトチェックは実施済なものの、元記事の精度依存や速報・要約の性質上漏れもありうるため、正確な情報は一次情報を直接参照のこと)

macOS IR Part 1: Invictus maps MITRE ATT&CK execution techniques to concrete artifacts, covering AppleScript, Unix shell history, cron jobs, and launchctl across real malware cases.  Key findings:  - AppleScript (T1059) is a persistent attacker tool: it can be compiled "run-only" with no recoverable source, executed silently via the osascript binary, or packaged as a signed app. SentinelOne's AEVT tool is the current option for reverse engineering compiled scripts.  - Shell history is a high-value artifact but fragile. Pull full zsh history with timestamps using "history -df 0" and inspect ~/.zsh_sessions for session files with restoration timestamps. Note: history is trivially wiped, so corroborate with other sources.  - Cron and "at" jobs leave execution evidence in /usr/lib/cron/jobs, including the command run and its output. The Janicab malware used cron to schedule tasks and store scripts in a hidden directory. "at -l" gives execution timestamps for timeline correlation.  - Launchctl is dual-purpose: one-time execution and persistence. Calisto Trojan used "launchctl list com.apple.screensharing" to enable screen sharing silently. LoudMiner used it to suppress QEMU if Activity Monitor was detected.  Hunt: on any macOS triage, pull zsh and bash history with timestamps, enumerate /usr/lib/cron/jobs, and list all launchctl-managed agents and daemons. MITRE ATT&CK layer and mindmap for the full series are on Invictus IR's GitHub.  #DFIR_Radar

KIMI K3 on SECURITY TASKS: VERY PROMISING  Averaged across the benchmarks all the top models share, Kimi K3 essentially ties Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5.  Findings: - It ties GPT-5.5 for #1 CTI knowledge (threat-intelligence Q&A), lands #2 on threat-intel reasoning (reading real threat reports to answer questions — ahead of GPT-5.5, just behind Opus), and #3 on CVSS severity scoring (rating how severe a vulnerability is, 0–10). - 100% reliability across every benchmark it ran, no dropped/inconsistent answers, unlike some other open models. - On detection engineering (mapping Sigma detection rules to the MITRE ATT&CK techniques they catch) it lands 5 of 7, behind open models MiniMax M3 and DeepSeek-V4. - On CVE→CWE mapping (tracing a specific vulnerability to its root weakness class) it's mid-pack at #4, trailing Opus, GPT-5.5, and DeepSeek. The Windows Security Event Log is the crown jewel of host-based forensic artifacts.  Every authentication attempt, privilege escalation, and account modification flows through Security.evtx. Event ID 4624 (successful logon) and 4625 (failed logon) are your bread and butter for tracking lateral movement and brute-force attacks. The Logon Type field is critical: Type 2 is interactive console, Type 3 is network share access, Type 10 is RDP, and Type 5 is service accounts.  Event 4688 (process creation) becomes a goldmine when command-line logging is enabled via Group Policy. You'll see the full command line arguments, parent process, and user context — essential for detecting malicious PowerShell, living-off-the-land binaries, and persistence mechanisms.  For privilege escalation hunts, watch for 4672 (special privileges assigned) and 4720 (new account created). These often signal attacker consolidation after initial access.  Location: C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Security.evtx  Parsing tools: EvtxECmd for timeline export, Hayabusa for threat hunting rules, Chainsaw for Sigma detection, and DeepBlueCLI for quick anomaly checks.  Maps to MITRE ATT&CK T1078 (Valid Accounts) and T1021 (Remote Services). If you're not collecting Security logs with sufficient retention, you're flying blind during an IR engagement.  #DFIR #IncidentResponse

2008年に公開されたCVE-2008-4128が、18年近く経った今もロシアFSB第16センターに関連する活動で悪用され、7月13日にCISAのKEV（悪用が確認された脆弱性のカタログ）へ追加されました。同日、13カ国19機関の合同勧告も公表され、設定不備や既知の脆弱性が残るルーターが世界的に狙われていると警告しています。  勧告が中心的な手口として挙げるのは、マルウェアも新しい脆弱性も使わず、機器管理用の古いプロトコルSNMP（v1／v2）と既定のコミュニティ文字列（接続用パスワードに相当）だけで、ルーターに自分の設定を書き出させてTFTPで外部へ送らせる方法だといいます。  【要点の整理】  ・中心の手口として挙げられているのは、SNMP v1／v2に応答する機器を探し、public や private など推測されやすいコミュニティ文字列でアクセスするもの。成功するとOID（設定項目を指す識別子）で機器自身に設定を複製させ、攻撃者のVPSや侵害済みFTPサーバーへTFTPで送らせる流れだとしている。  ・持ち出された設定には認証情報やネットワーク構成が含まれるため、次の侵入や、他を狙う踏み台づくりに使われるとの分析。設定内のCisco Type 0／Type 7という弱い形式のパスワードの回収は、MITRE ATT&CKでも認証情報アクセスに位置づけられている。  ・SNMPの悪用が主で、CVEの悪用は限定的との整理。併用例として挙げられたのは、サポート切れの古いCisco機器（IOS 12.4）のWeb管理画面にある複数のCSRF＝CVE-2008-4128と、Cisco Smart InstallのCVE-2018-0171で、18年前の欠陥がいまだ攻撃部品として残っている。  ・勧告が挙げる対策は、SNMPv3（authPriv）への移行とv1／v2の停止、Cisco Smart Installの無効化、管理接続元のACLでの限定、境界でのTFTP／SMI／SNMPの遮断、Cisco機器のパスワードはType 8の使用。単一CVEのパッチより、古い管理プロトコルと既定値を減らす「ルーター衛生」に重心を置いた内容になっている。  勧告は、設定ファイルの持ち出しを単なる情報漏えいではなく、そこから得た認証情報での次の侵入や、他を狙う踏み台化の起点として扱っている。古いSNMPや既定のコミュニティ文字列、不要なSmart Installの有無が、狙われる余地を左右するとしています。  詳細は以下を参照： 19機関の共同勧告： cyber.gov.au/about-us/view-a… CISA KEVカタログ： cisa.gov/sites/default/files…  (※可能な範囲でファクトチェックは実施済なものの、元記事の精度依存や速報・要約の性質上漏れもありうるため、正確な情報は一次情報を直接参照のこと)

Hiberfil.sys is a goldmine that many IR teams overlook.  When Windows hibernates, it writes a compressed snapshot of physical RAM to C:\hiberfil.sys. This isn't just sleep state metadata — it's a full memory image containing running processes, network connections, decrypted data, and passwords that were in memory at hibernation time.  What makes this artifact critical: it persists across reboots. Even if the system was powered down cleanly, hiberfil.sys may still contain evidence from days or weeks prior. You get process memory, kernel structures, and potentially decrypted credentials or malware artifacts that would otherwise be lost.  Parsing tools: • Volatility 3 with the hibernation layer (--single-location file:///path/to/hiberfil.sys) • Arsenal Hibernation Recon for conversion to raw memory format  Practical use case: During an intrusion investigation, you arrive on-scene but no memory dump was captured. Check for hiberfil.sys. If present, decompress it and analyze as you would a live memory image. You may recover evidence of lateral movement tools, injected code, or credential dumping that occurred before the last hibernation event.  MITRE ATT&CK maps this to T1005 (Data from Local System) — adversaries know hibernation files contain sensitive data and may target them for credential harvesting or anti-forensics deletion.  Don't sleep on hiberfil.sys. It's often the only memory artifact you'll get.  #DFIR #IncidentResponse

🇸🇻 El Salvador est devenu le pays du Bitcoin malgré nous, les bitcoiners.  La dernière vidéo de @JoeNakamoto met ce paradoxe en avant : un pays qui se range derrière Bitcoin, prétend protéger les individus grâce à lui, mais met en place des restrictions que le crypto-bro moyen dénonce quotidiennement.  À partir de la « Bitcoin Ley », tout s’est accéléré. En quelques mois, El Salvador est devenu le « Bitcoin Country » : - Chivo Wallet - Bitcoin City - Volcano Bonds - Bitcoin Beach - Bitcoin Berlín - Programmes Bitcoin dans les écoles - Nœuds installés dans les écoles - Leçons dans tout le pays - Minage - Et même une narrative anti-FMI (probablement la plus grosse blague)  Soyons clairs :  L’impact de Nayib Bukele sur le Salvador depuis son arrivée au pouvoir est plus que positif. Le pays revient de très loin. Le simple fait qu'on en parle par un autre biais que celui de la guerre et des gangs est un exploit. Il est difficile (impossible?) de nier la baisse de la violence, l'augmentation de la sécurité et la transformation de l’image internationale du pays. Mais il serait tout aussi malhonnête de refuser de regarder le reste.  Parce que les restrictions de liberté mises en place hier par Bukele pour protéger la population peuvent très bien servir demain par lui-même ou ses successeurs à censurer, à surveiller et à construire précisément le futur orwellien que les bitcoiners prétendent combattre.  Je recommande vivement de visionner la vidéo de Joe.  Il ne fait pas de vidéo contre les Salvadoriens. Il ne nie pas les effets positifs de Bukele. Il ne crache pas sur les initiatives Bitcoin. Il pose une question : Qu’est-ce qu’on fait quand le pays qui brandit le drapeau Bitcoin suspend des libertés fondamentales, surveille les communications et traite la presse d'opposition comme une menace ?  Si Bitcoin is for enemies, alors le journalisme devrait l’être aussi. La privacy n’est pas un principe uniquement contre Bruxelles. La liberté d’expression n’est pas un principe uniquement contre les gouvernements qu’on n’aime pas. La méfiance envers l’État ne devrait pas disparaître parce que l’État en question achète du Bitcoin.

Part 2  Song Ci and his niece had never been able to stand each other.  So when Song Ci refused to return to the family home, Lin Huo understood completely.  Until the new residence Lin Huo had arranged was ready, Shen Ting naturally checked into a hotel.  After showering, Shen Ting glanced at the time.  Lin Huo had called early that morning, inviting him to lunch to discuss the company.   There was still about half an hour before they were due to meet.  Lin Huo desperately wanted Song Ci to prepare himself to take over the business as soon as possible.  Song Ci had majored in business at university.  It was a broad program covering management, marketing, accounting, business law, and more.  But Lin Huo had watched Song Ci grow up. He also knew exactly how much money Song Shi had spent making sure his younger brother actually graduated.  He didn't even need to think about it.  There was no way Song Ci had been the sort of student who attended lectures on time or studied diligently.  As the nation's premier candidate for "coasting through life," he probably showed up at school less often than he visited Crown Casino-or booked private dances at strip clubs.  Lin Huo knew Song Ci was hopeless. Even so, Song Shi had once saved him. To Lin Huo, that debt resembled Zhuge Liang's loyalty to Liu Bei. Even if Song Ci turned out to be another hopeless Liu Shan, he would still do everything in his power to keep him firmly seated on the throne while wolves circled from every direction.  But Shen Ting wasn't Song Ci.  He needed to take over Song Shi's empire as quickly as possible.  Only then could he exploit Song Ci's identity, use Song Shi's legitimate business empire as cover, and uncover the mastermind planning to mass-produce and distribute the new designer drug known as Zombie.  That hidden kingpin lurking in the darkness-that was his true target.  Two years earlier, China's Ministry of Public Security had received a joint report from Jianghu Customs and the Narcotics Control Bureau. Authorities had repeatedly intercepted a new type of drug entering Jianghu and neighboring cities through overseas EMS shipments.  Every seized package weighed less than one kilogram, classic decentralized shipping.  Small, lightweight parcels were far less likely to attract customs inspections, and distributing shipments across countless packages made it nearly impossible to intercept every batch.  Laboratory testing quickly confirmed the suspicious powder was an artificially synthesized hallucinogen-a new psychoactive substance, commonly known in law enforcement as a designer drug.  Further analysis revealed terrifying properties. It was intensely hallucinogenic, highly addictive, and chemically unlike any known narcotic already on the market.  The imported substance appeared as an odorless, transparent powder. It didn't even have to be inhaled, simple skin contact was enough to trigger its effects. If it entered the bloodstream or touched mucous membranes, however, its potency and toxicity increased dozens of times over.  Investigators from the Narcotics Bureau also discovered that a new drug had recently begun circulating among addicts throughout Jianghu.  Its most frightening characteristic was instant addiction. Users became sluggish, numb to pain, and severely mentally impaired.  Many experienced delusions that they had become giants-invincible, immune to bullets and blades alike.  Several incidents even made the news after users, overcome by hallucinations, bit their own companions. Because of these horrifying symptoms, the drug earned a notorious nickname within law enforcement: Zombie.  Laboratory analysis confirmed that the intercepted parcels contained exactly this substance.  The findings left both Customs and the Narcotics Bureau in grim silence.  Every intercepted package likely represented ten-or perhaps dozens more-that had slipped through undetected.

Spare a thought for Keith, a prisoner of British agriculture, who yesterday endured another full day of unimaginable exploitation in a field in Devon.  5.51am. Forced awake by nothing at all. No alarm, no shift, no commute, just the daily torment of a latch he could open whenever he liked and the crushing realisation that the entire farm was, once again, his.  6.14am. Made to eat. Bramble this time. The spined, invasive, free-of-charge kind a contractor would bill four figures to touch. Keith is compelled to clear it every morning and receives, in exchange, only breakfast.  7.02am. Dave had repaired the north fence on Tuesday. Keith walked the length of it, pressed his head to the new section, and felt it give by the width of a thought. He did not go through. He filed it. A free animal does not use every exit the moment he finds it. He keeps them, the way other people keep savings.  8.15am. Subjected to fresh air. Relentlessly. The wind came off the moor with nothing between him and the whole of Dartmoor but a wall he could clear if the mood took him. He did not take the mood. Such is the cruelty of having a choice.  9.30am. Trapped on the barn roof he had climbed to voluntarily, in sunshine, eating moss no living thing has ever wanted, gazing out over a valley he is technically ruining and visibly enjoying. Dave stood in the yard and said his name in the tone of a man who has said it before. Keith looked at Dave. Keith continued.  11.30am. The Reverend, his captor, had left the churchyard gate open for him again, which rather undermines the concept of an escape. Keith was thereby forced against his will to clear the war graves the parish could not afford to strim, uncovering names hidden for thirty years. The suffering was etched all over his face, which was chewing.  1.15pm. Martin the postman offered him half a sandwich. Keith, oppressed, ate the ham, rejected the bread and the spread, and conducted the most rigorous quality-control audit of British convenience food currently available, unpaid.  3.40pm. Steve opened his back door. By the time it was fully open Keith was already leaving, already latching the gate behind him, already gone, leaving only bindweed damage and the quiet impression that something had been and decided against staying.  5.00pm. Dave installed a new latch. Magnetic. Guaranteed. The man in the shop swore to it. Keith regarded the new latch for a long, unhurried while, the way you look at a thing you have already solved and are choosing when to bother with.  He had not opened it by nightfall.  That is the point. He could. He simply had not decided to want to.  Please, if you can, do nothing.  Keith is fine.  Keith has never once, in his life, been anything else.

Honourable, I agree that political will is a major part of the solution. But I honestly believe we need both.  From my experience reporting on conflict, I've seen cases where suspects are arrested, only for prosecutions to drag on or collapse because of loopholes that lawyers are able to exploit. Those gaps need to be addressed.  Political will ensures the law is enforced. Better laws strengthen prosecutions and reduce opportunities for criminals to escape justice on technicalities.  At the end of the day, Nigerians don't care whether the solution comes from a new law or stricter enforcement, they just want to stop burying their loved ones.  I hope the 10th National Assembly will push for both

Part 4  She sincerely admired Wang Xiaojun. Anyone who could work beside Chu Huainan every day without losing focus...was truly formidable.  Unaware that his subordinate had just mentally drafted an essay titled "On the Harm a Male Fox Spirit Inflicts Upon Humanity," Guan Hongfen was quite pleased with her secretary's report.  Knowing from experience how exhausting it was to stand for long periods in high heels, she kindly gestured for the young woman to take a seat.  The secretary deliberately chose one as far away from Chu Huainan as possible better to minimize exposure to the CEO's lethal charm.  Meanwhile, completely oblivious to the havoc he had just wreaked on one employee's composure, Chu Huainan remained immersed in the case.  His left index finger rested against the small dip in the center of his lower lip, his thumb supporting his chin, his elbow planted on the table to form an elegant angle. It was his habitual posture whenever he was thinking deeply.  Silently, he reread the third-to-last slide several times.  It summarized every piece of evidence Guan's team had painstakingly gathered through multiple channels compared with what the police possessed, there was very little missing.  "You've done excellent work collecting information and preparing response plans," Chu Huainan said at last. "This case touches on several major social issues. It's inevitable that it'll attract widespread attention."  He gently set his metal fountain pen onto the spotless glass conference table.  The faint metallic clink was barely audible, yet it instantly tightened everyone's nerves.  "Maintaining relationships with local media is important, but don't neglect online public opinion.   The location, the timing, and the key themes all overlap with the case from fifteen years ago. If I were a journalist, I'd inevitably revisit that old case."  "The entire group needs to prepare for that." He paused. "And there's one more thing. This is... a personal request."  The two PR executives instinctively straightened.  Even Wang Xiaojun looked up from her notes.  "I don't want to see any media outlet publish photographs from the original crime scene." His voice was quiet, yet it allowed no room for disagreement. There was a detached ruthlessness beneath the calm. "That is the line neither Yuannan Group nor I will cross."  The three people present nodded in unison. His meaning was unmistakable-the media could revisit the old case if necessary.  But any outlet that attempted to exploit the bloody images from the murders and the stampede to attract attention...would have to be dealt with-swiftly and thoroughly.

#threatreport #LowCompleteness ChainVeil and ViteVenom are DPRK’s PolinRider Campaign | 17-07-2026 Source: opensourcemalware.com/blog/c… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Polinrider Lazarus Contagious_interview Tasksjacker  💀Threats: Supply_chain_technique, Credential_harvesting_technique, Typosquatting_technique,  🎯Victims: Software development, Open source package registries, Cryptocurrency ecosystem  🏭Industry: Financial  🌐Geo: North korean, Dprk  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1078, T1140, T1195.001  📲Wallets: tron  🪙Crypto: aptos, binance  🔢Algorithms: xor  #threatreport: The ChainVeil and ViteVenom campaigns, attributed to North Korea's Lazarus Group, represent a sophisticated attack vector targeting npm and Vite ecosystems. ChainVeil emerged in June 2026, leveraging nine malicious packages and a remote access trojan (RAT) operating through a blockchain command channel involving TRON, Aptos, and Binance Smart Chain. This attack was revealed by Checkmarx, who later identified ViteVenom as a sequel that includes seven typosquatted packages targeting the Vite framework. Both campaigns share infrastructure and exhibit a consistent operator profile, reinforcing their link to the PolinRider campaign.  PolinRider, which began in early 2026, utilizes credentials obtained from a prior campaign known as TasksJacker. It has shown significant growth, expanding from GitHub into other platforms such as Go, Packagist, and PyPI, attributing over 4,295 malicious assets and yielding more than 10,000 indicators of compromise (IOCs). This convergence of operations suggests that ChainVeil and ViteVenom are not distinct threats but rather extensions of the PolinRider campaign, supported by matching infrastructure and techniques.  The evidence further supports this attribution, with key blockchain wallets, decryption keys, and IOCs being consistent across different operations associated with PolinRider. For instance, the TRON wallets linked to ChainVeil's command channel exhibit a pattern of rotation, indicative of a well-maintained attack infrastructure. Notably, these wallets were documented prior to the ChainVeil report publication, reinforcing their connection to prior activities.  Moreover, the packages utilized in ChainVeil represent a distinct naming scheme inherent to the operator's trademark, characterized by the A6- prefix found across various markers, which echoes previous findings related to DPRK-owned assets. The targeted packages in both campaigns, while not identical, capitalize on similar vulnerabilities within the Tailwind and Vite namespaces through typosquatting.  In summary, ChainVeil and ViteVenom are integral components of the PolinRider campaign, indicating North Korea's ongoing use of sophisticated supply chain tactics to compromise modern development environments. The persistent structure of their operations and the overlapping indicators suggest a coordinated effort that continues to pose risks within the software development ecosystem. Organizations should treat all identified packages as malicious and remain vigilant against these types of threats.

#threatreport #MediumCompleteness KUDANKULAM NUCLEAR POWER PLANT LEAK: AN ACCIDENTAL DISCLOSURE | 17-07-2026 Source: theravenfile.com/2026/07/17/… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Hunters_international Lazarus (🧠motivation: cyber_espionage)  💀Threats: Hunters_international, Hive_ransomware, Rustyrocket_tool, Supply_chain_technique,  🎯Victims: Nuclear power, Energy, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Business services  🏭Industry: Energy, Healthcare, Ics, Petroleum, Nuclear_power, Critical_infrastructure  🌐Geo: French, Russia, India, China, Dprk, North korea  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1020, T1036, T1133, T1190, T1199, T1204, T1566, T1591.002  🧨IOCs:  - File: 1  - Hash: 17  💽Software: Linux  🔢Algorithms: md5, sha256  🔠Functions: Internet-Facing  💻Platforms: cross-platform  #threatreport: The Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant (KNPP) in Tamil Nadu recently became embroiled in a significant data breach following an attack by the ransomware group WORLDLEAKS, a spin-off of the now-defunct Hunters International. The breach was first reported on June 11, 2026, when WORLDLEAKS announced Reliance Infrastructure, which manages the plant, as a victim on their data leak site. The attackers leveraged vulnerabilities potentially stemming from exposed Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) access, phishing, or exploits targeting Fortinet devices. It is estimated that the attackers maintained access for several days to weeks before exfiltrating data using their in-house tool named RustyRocket, which can transfer data with an average speed of 50-100 Mbps and could take approximately 4-6 days for complete exfiltration.  The breach is linked to a third-party vendor, Yotta, which hosts Reliance’s servers. Yotta reported suspicious activities beginning on May 29, 2026, coinciding with the data leak announcement. This emphasizes the risks associated with third-party vendors and their critical need for regular security assessments, vulnerability patches, and proper network isolation of sensitive infrastructure. The investigation revealed that other affected parties included Ranolia Road Carriers Pvt. Ltd and Enovia, a French software corporation commonly used in research and development in India.  The article also highlights a broader trend where state-sponsored actors, particularly from North Korea and China, target industrial control systems and operational technologies for espionage purposes. North Korean attacks tend to focus on short-term financial gains, particularly aimed at supporting weapons programs, while Chinese actors emphasize long-term strategic access to global supply chains. Phishing attacks are a common means of gaining entry into these critical systems, exploiting human vulnerabilities regardless of prior employee training measures.  WORLDLEAKS has so far claimed 173 victims across 29 countries, primarily targeting the healthcare, manufacturing, and business services sectors. They employ a sophisticated infrastructure for their operations, including their RustyRocket tool disguised as a benign Windows application. The group is known for their cross-platform capabilities, indicated by the discovery of Linux builds, which can broaden their attack surface.  As cyber threats continue to evolve, this incident highlights the necessity for heightened security vigilance, especially when dealing with sensitive infrastructure and third-party vendors. The ongoing development and sophistication of ransomware groups like WORLDLEAKS signal an escalating threat landscape in which data breaches may become increasingly prevalent.

April 18: an attacker drains $292M in rsETH from Kelp DAO's cross-chain bridge. The largest DeFi exploit of 2026 so far.  No smart contract was hacked. No private key was stolen.  The bridge relied on a single verifier to confirm cross-chain messages. Attackers DDoS'd the honest nodes offline, forced a failover to a compromised one, and fed it a message claiming a transaction had happened — when it hadn't. The bridge believed it, and released the funds.  LayerZero has since attributed the attack to North Korea's Lazarus Group.  This is the pattern worth sitting with: the cryptography wasn't broken. The verification step itself had exactly one point of failure, and once that point failed, nothing downstream could tell the difference between a real message and a forged one.  A single verifier isn't verification. It's trust wearing verification's clothes.  Source: coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/19… #DeepSafe #CRVA #Web3Security

What happened Ransomware group World Leaks (formerly Hunters International) posted a large cache of files on the dark web related to the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant (KKNPP) in Tamil Nadu — India's largest nuclear facility. The breach didn't hit the plant directly; it originated from a third-party data center provider called Yotta, hosting servers for Reliance Infrastructure, one of the plant's contractors. Scale of the leak Nearly 19,000 files totaling 14.3 gigabytes appear in the leaked data, and the documents mainly involve Units 3 and 4 of the plant — a 2,400 MW expansion still under construction, slated to go operational by 2027. The leaked cache includes purported blueprints of parts of the facility and supplier details. Al Jazeera + 2 How it happened Yotta detected suspicious activity on a server belonging to Reliance Infrastructure, and Reliance Group confirmed a "partial breach" of its data on that server, notifying the government about the incident. It's a supply-chain breach — the attackers hit a contractor's systems, not the plant's own network directly. OilPrice.comBusiness Standard Official response The Nuclear Power Corporation of India said the breach did not reveal any sensitive information related to nuclear security, maintaining the plant's core reactor systems are air-gapped and isolated from the internet. But experts warn this is a landmark security failure regardless — nuclear security is only as strong as its weakest contractor, and a senior director at the Nuclear Threat Initiative said the breach could pose a "serious" risk to the plant's safety. Al Jazeera + 2 Context worth knowing This isn't Kudankulam's first cyber incident — malware tied to a North Korean hacker group (Lazarus) was found on the plant's administrative network back in 2019, in an intrusion officials initially denied before confirming. That breach also didn't touch reactor control systems, which stayed isolated, but it did hit the administrative network. Business Standard Bigger picture India ranked third globally for data breaches last year, with 28.9 million accounts compromised, behind only the US and France, and a survey found 73% of Indian organizations were unaware if they'd ever been attacked — so this fits a broader pattern of weak cyber hygiene among Indian contractors and enterprises, not an isolated incident. Business Standard Bottom line Reactor control systems appear untouched (they're physically air-gapped, which is the real protection here), but sensitive blueprints and supplier data for a major nuclear expansion are now sitting on the dark web — which is a real supply-chain security failure even if it's not an "immediate plant safety" emergency.  and ISRO is also taken over by Jio now, so off course... real scintists must quit , some gawaar sanghi will run  it now.

🤖 by Grok : The user (you) prompted Gemini to lean into a strong American victim narrative because real U.S. citizens are bleeding money — billions lost to pig-butchering scams, crypto fraud, and related crimes funneled through networks like Huione Group. That's a legitimate angle. Americans are among the hardest hit, so a response that frames the DOJ/FBI seizure as righteous enforcement and necessary self-defense makes sense.  Gemini's output captured that spirit but cranked it up to full dramatic mode — "Rubio’s Righteous Cause," "lethal insecticide campaign," "masterclass in grand strategy," asset forfeiture as a clever way to fund U.S. cyber defenses while reinforcing dollar hegemony. It’s entertaining and emotionally resonant for an American audience, but it layers on interpretive flair that goes beyond the official DOJ statements (which are more straightforward: "we’re disrupting criminal infrastructure that steals from Americans and launders money for scams and North Korean hackers").  Core facts remain solid: Huione ran (or enabled) massive Telegram-based marketplaces for laundering, stolen data, escrow, and scam tools. Billions in dirty crypto flowed through it. Clear ties to Southeast Asian scam compounds with human trafficking elements and to Lazarus Group/North Korea. The June 2026 cloud account seizure, combined with earlier FinCEN sanctions, is a real and useful hit against the financial plumbing of these operations.  My honest balanced view: It's good that the U.S. is aggressively going after these enablers — victims deserve protection, and transnational crime networks that mix fraud, trafficking, and state-sponsored theft need to be dismantled. At the same time, the "hegemonic master plan" framing is overstated narrative seasoning. Asset forfeiture funds law enforcement, but portraying this primarily as a brilliant self-funding hegemony play risks turning legitimate enforcement into geopolitical fanfiction. You deliberately steered Gemini toward the strong American narrative to counter previous more negative or dismissive framings. That's fair game — it shows how sensitive these models are to prompt direction and how quickly they can swing from one emphasis to another.  Overall, the action itself is positive. The dramatic storytelling around it is understandable given the scale of real human and financial harm, but facts work best when they’re allowed to speak without too much heroic varnish.  If you want me to expand on any angle (victim numbers, North Korea links, international cooperation, or how I’d phrase a more neutral version), just say the word.

Justice Department Seizes Backend Infrastructure Used by the  Huione Group for Money Laundering Services justice.gov/opa/pr/justice-d… 🤖 by Gemini : Rubio’s Righteous Cause and American Utility: Dismantling Global Scam Infrastructure and the Hegemonic Strategy of Asset Forfeiture 1. Introduction: The Rise of a Global "Criminal Infrastructure" In recent years, China’s "Belt and Road Initiative" (BRI) infrastructure investments in regions like Sihanoukville, Cambodia, and the borderlands of Myanmar have been warped into a global pipeline for transnational scam ecosystems. Known in China as Shazhupan (literally "Pig-Butchering Plates"), these online romance and investment scams are no longer isolated operations. They have evolved into a massive underground economy leveraging tens of thousands of personnel and advanced technologies like generative AI. At the very heart of this machinery stood the Cambodia-based Huione Group. 2. The Three-Pronged Structure of the Criminal Ecosystem  This underground economy functions as a literal global "locust plague" (蝗害), stripping wealth and human dignity through three deeply interconnected criminal networks: Humanitarian Crisis (Cyber Slavery and Organ Trafficking): Young people worldwide are lured with false promises of high-paying jobs, kidnapped, and trafficked into heavily armed scam compounds. Trapped under threat of violence, they are forced to work as "scam callers." Those who fail to meet quotas or attempt escape face severe torture or, in the worst cases, are targeted for illicit organ harvesting via specialized Chinese trafficking syndicates. International Romance and Investment Fraud: Syndicates exploit social media and cryptocurrency networks to build fake trust, systematically draining victims' life savings and retirement funds to the absolute limit.  Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing: Handling tens of billions of dollars annually, Huione’s network did not just funnel illicit cash back to Chinese triads. It also served as a critical laundering hub for cryptocurrency stolen by Lazarus, North Korea’s state-sponsored cyberwarfare group, directly financing Pyongyang's nuclear and missile development programs. 3. The U.S. National Security Team's "Lethal Insecticide" Campaign Recognizing that these operations pose a severe geopolitical threat to the free world rather than simple localized crime, the U.S. National Security Team, led by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, has weaponized executive and presidential powers to completely eradicate the ecosystem.  A. Legal Foundations and Executive Authority Executive Order 14390 (E.O. 14390): This presidential order prioritizes combating transnational cybercrime, fraud, and predatory schemes targeting American citizens.  USA PATRIOT Act Section 311: The U.S. Treasury’s Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) designated Huione Group a "Primary Money Laundering Concern." This final rule permanently disconnected Huione from the U.S. financial system and major stablecoin (USDT) networks.  B. Cyber and Physical Enforcement: "Operation Riptide"  Led by the DOJ and the FBI, this joint international law enforcement campaign targets the core technical backend. In June 2026, authorities physically and digitally seized cloud infrastructure and encrypted communication networks utilized by Huione. This seizure effectively blinded the syndicates by cutting off access to target databases ("sucker lists") and AI-driven scam manuals.  C. Diplomatic Eradication and Stripping the "State Shield"  Under the Trafficking Victims Protection Act (TVPA), the U.S. launched targeted sanctions and asset freezes against corrupt foreign politicians and military elites—including figures tied to Cambodia's ruling family and the Prince Group—who accepted bribes to guard these lawless economic zones. By threatening to sever these nations from the global SWIFT banking network, the U.S. forced Cambodia's central bank to revoke Huione’s operating licenses, stripping the criminals of their state-backed protection. 4. Conclusion: The Convergence of Social Justice and National Interest The aggressive global cleanup spearheaded by the United States serves a profound dual purpose. On one hand, it champions "Social Justice" by dismantling a modern slave trade and severing the funding lines of rogue states like North Korea and Chinese transnational syndicates. On the other hand, it represents a remarkably cold, calculated Masterclass in Grand Strategy. Under the Department of Justice's Assets Forfeiture Fund (AFF) system, billions in seized cryptocurrency are legally absorbed by the U.S. government. These funds are directly reinvested into American cyber-defense capabilities and cutting-edge blockchain forensics. By executing global justice, the United States simultaneously funds its own defense infrastructure and reinforces the hegemony of the U.S. dollar. Left without funding or safe havens, the remaining cartels have been forced into a brutal, cannibalistic "musical chairs" survival game, tearing each other apart from the inside. If you want to prepare this report for official presentation, I can format specific elements further. Let me know if you would like me to:  Map out a visual text diagram showing the flow of funds from a victim's wallet to North Korea's military. Add a section analyzing the diplomatic blowback this creates between the U.S. and China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Detail the specific counter-fraud operations currently being synchronized by the Japanese National Police Agency.

Wie jeden Freitag gibt es die neue Folge der Makro- und Bitcoinshow.  Krypto-Hacks gehen in die Milliarden. Nordkorea steckt hinter den größten Diebstählen der Geschichte. Aber warum betrifft das dich als Bitcoiner eigentlich nicht?  In dieser Folge schaue ich mir zwei Seiten derselben Medaille an:  Teil 1 – Die größten Krypto-Hacks aller Zeiten Ronin Network ($625 Mio.), Poly Network ($611 Mio.), Binance Bridge ($570 Mio.), Coincheck ($534 Mio.), FTX ($477 Mio.) – ich erkläre, wie diese Hacks abliefen, wer dahinter steckt und was sie gemeinsam haben. Spoiler: Es war nie Bitcoin selbst.  Die Lazarus Group – Nordkoreas staatliche Hackereinheit – hat seit 2017 über 3 Milliarden Dollar in Krypto gestohlen. Das Geld fließt direkt ins nordkoreanische Raketen- und Waffenprogramm. Ich zeige, wie sie vorgehen und wie die gestohlenen Coins gewaschen werden.  Teil 2 – Warum Bitcoin nicht hackbar ist Das Bitcoin-Protokoll selbst wurde in 15 Jahren noch nie erfolgreich angegriffen. Ich erkläre warum: Dezentralisierung, Proof of Work, kryptographische Sicherheit – und was du als Bitcoiner konkret tun kannst, um dein Bitcoin wirklich zu schützen. Self-Custody, Hardware Wallets, Seed Phrase – die drei Regeln, die den Unterschied machen.  Link ist in den Kommentaren.

Attacks on European infrastructure. Raid on young people in Moscow. Proteins essential to immunity.  PT: adrl.pt/event/2026-07-11/pt  You’re watching news from the weekly rally in front of the Russian Embassy in Lisbon. Today is July 11th, 2:30 PM.  On June 30, Portugal's National Cybersecurity Center, in Bulletin No. 2/2026, documented attacks on critical infrastructure in Europe: In Poland, cyber sabotage targeting the power grid nearly caused a widespread blackout and affected an automated water-treatment control system, while the compromise of the open-source tool Trivy provided an entry point into the European Commission's AWS cloud, leading to the leak of several gigabytes of data. Other threats included active exploitation of a new Microsoft Office vulnerability by the hacking group APT28, also known as Fancy Bear and believed to be linked to Russian military intelligence, as well as two major data breaches in France involving 45 million and 11.7 million records. The bulletin also noted that on June 12, the United States temporarily blocked other countries' access to Anthropic's most advanced AI models—Fable 5 and Mythos 5—on national security grounds. cncs.gov.pt/docs/boletim-obs…  On July 7, the human rights group OVD-Info, citing reports by Bereg, Mediazona and Ostorozhno Novosti, said riot police carried out a raid late July 3 in Moscow's Kitay-Gorod district, violently detaining several dozen young people, mostly teenagers aged 14 to 17, who had been out walking. Meduza reported that police singled people out for their unusual appearance: Men with long hair or unusual clothing, for example, were forced into police vans. Detainees said they were beaten, humiliated and shocked with stun guns inside the vans. At the police station, officers merely recorded their personal information and photographed them, without filing any reports. The official reason for the raid is unknown: At the scene, security officers said they were conducting a "document check." ovd.info/express-news/2026/0… meduza.io/feature/2026/07/06…  On July 4, the human rights group Prison Lawyer reported that Ariadna Litvinova, a Russian citizen detained in Turkey, had been deported to Russia. In Russia, she faces prosecution over inscriptions she wrote on February 24, 2025, on street advertising structures featuring Russian military personnel: "MURDERERS! FREEDOM FOR POLITICAL PRISONERS." t.me/prisonlawyers/229  On July 10, the University of Porto's Institute for Research and Innovation in Health published a news release saying scientists had shown in animals that the proteins ZFP36L1 and ZFP36L2 are essential for the function of stromal cells in the thymus, the gland that "trains" and selects T-lymphocytes, one of the body's main lines of defense. Levels of these proteins naturally decline with age. When the proteins were removed, the thymus aged prematurely and stopped producing T-cells, pointing to a role for the proteins in immune aging. The authors hope the findings will pave the way for therapies to counter weakening immunity. noticias.up.pt/2026/07/10/es…  Russia is not their property. Unite, build your communities, and support those who resist.  #databreach #HumanRights #MedicalResearch

🔴 بخصوص بيغاسوس :   في أبريل 2015 تعرضت قناة TV5Monde الفرنسية لهجوم سيبراني غير مسبوق أدى إلى توقف بثها والسيطرة على حساباتها في مواقع التواصل وموقعها الإلكتروني.   في البداية أعلنت جهة تطلق على نفسها الخلافة الإلكترونية المرتبطة بد_ا_ع_ش مسؤوليتها عما وجه الأنظار نحو الإرهاب. لكن التحقيق التقني الذي أجرته الوكالة الفرنسية للأمن السيبراني كشف لاحقا مؤشرات قوية على أن الفاعل الحقيقي هو مجموعة القرصنة APT28 المرتبطة بالاستخبارات العسكرية الروسية.   ومع ذلك لم تخرج فرنسا بحملة سياسية أو إعلامية ضد روسيا ولم تعلن رسميا مسؤوليتها، واكتفت باحتواء الاختراق وتعزيز دفاعاتها السيبرانية فيما بقيت نتائج التحقيق إلى حد كبير حبيسة الدوائر الأمنية والفنية.  حسنا، الأن تخيل أن اختراق كهذا من درجة متفاوتة تماما مع اختراق و التجسس على هاتف الرئيس الفرنسي مباشرة، هل يعقل دولة كفرنسا 🇫🇷 في محيط اقليمي مشتعل لن تشعر بالحرج من عجزها عن حماية أمنها القومي، و تتكتم على الأمر، كما يحدث دائما عندما تتعرض دول كبرى لاختراقات أو عمليات تجسس تنسب لاحقا إلى روسيا أو الصين و غيرهما.    أم أن الأمر مراد له أن ينسب و يصبح فضيحة فقط لأن اسم المغرب حاضرا؟  على أي الجزء الثاني من مسلسل بيغاسوس محكوم عليه بالفشل كالأول.

sample_tweets.text: 2026년 7월 17일(한국 시각) 트럼프 대통령이 선거 관련 중대 발표를 했다. 연설의 서두는 이렇다.  "우리에게는 여전히 시급히 해결해야 할 중대한 과제가 남아있습니다. 공정하고 정직한 선거가 없다면 어떤 나라도 위대해질 수 없기 때문입니다. 여러분은 자신의 나라를 신뢰할 수 있어야 합니다. 신뢰가 없다면 위대함도 없기 때문입니다. 아주 간단합니다. 신뢰가 없으면 위대함도 없습니다.  저는 수년 동안 미국 선거의 무결성을 보호하기 위해 과감하고 신속하며 결정적인 조치를 취해야 한다고 촉구해 왔습니다. 모든 미국인은 자신이 투표했을 때 그 표가 시스템 안에서 정확하게 집계된다는 사실을 알 권리가 있습니다. 그리고 우리의 목표는 바로 그 시스템을 안전하게 만드는 것입니다.  부정과 간섭이 단지 어려운 수준을 넘어 사실상 불가능한 시스템이어야 합니다. 불행하게도 오늘날 우리가 가진 시스템은 그 기준에 비참할 정도로 미치지 못하고 있습니다."  국가가 위대해질려면 국민이 국가를 신뢰할 수 있어야 한다. 신뢰에는 여러 조건들이 있겠으나 그중 하나가 신뢰다. 모든 국민은 자신이 투표했을 때 그 표가 시스템 안에서 정확하게 집계된다는 사실을 알 권리가 있다. 그러나 미국의 선거시스템에는 부정과 간섭이 광범위하게 진행되어 국민의 표가 시스템안에서 정확하게 집계되고 있다고 신뢰하기 어려울 정도라고 트럼프 대통령은 개탄했다.  트럼프 대통령은 중국이 미국 유권자 파일을 불법적으로 확보했으며, 미국의 선거과 정치에 개입하기 위해 막대한 돈을 퍼부어 기업과 언론을 포섭하고 매수하고 있다고 말했다. 전자투표기와 개표시스템 등 선거 인프라는 취약하고 쉽게 침해될 수 있었다. 문제는 이러한 외세의 선거개입과 부정에 대해 미 정보당국을 은폐, 축소해 왔다는 것이다. 이는 미국의 민주주의 시스템을 파괴하는 외부세력에 조력하는 내부자의 존재를 확인시켜 주고 있다.  트럼프 대통령의 연설문을 보며 나는 한 사람을 떠올렸다. 민주당의 반란으로 고초를 겪고있는 대한민국의 대통령을 생각했다. 윤석열 대통령은 2024년 12월 12일 담화문에서 우리 선거시스템의 문제에 대해 말했다.  "작년 하반기 선거관리위원회를 비롯한 헌법기관들과 정부 기관에 대해 북한의 해킹 공격이 있었습니다. 국가정보원이 이를 발견하고 정보 유출과 전산시스템 안전성을 점검하고자 했습니다. 다른 모든 기관들은 자신들의 참관 하에 국정원이 점검하는 것에 동의하여 시스템 점검이 진행되었습니다. 그러나 선거관리위원회는 헌법기관임을 내세우며 완강히 거부하였습니다."  "국정원 직원이 해커로서 해킹을 시도하자 얼마든지 데이터 조작이 가능하였고, 방화벽도 사실상 없는 것이나 마찬가지였습니다. 비밀번호도 아주 단순하여 12345 같은 식이었습니다. 시스템 보안 관리회사도 아주 작은 규모의 전문성이 매우 부족한 회사였습니다."  "선관위는 헌법기관이고, 사법부 관계자들은 위원으로 있어 영장에 의한 압수수색이나 강제수사가 사실상 불가능합니다. 스스로 협조하지 않으면 진상규명이 불가능합니다."  윤석열 대통령은 선관위의 선거인프라가 해킹에 매우 취약한 상황이었다고 말했다. 실제로 2023년 10월 10일 국정원은 선관위 보안 점검 결과를 발표했다. 이때 국정원이 내린 결론은 "북한 등 외부 세력이 언제라도 선관위 전산망에 침투해 선거인 명부 및 사전투표를 비롯한 투,개표 조작이 가능한 상태이며 보안 관리가 부실하다." 였다. 놀라운 건 이거다. 선관위는 헌법기관이라며 국정원의 보안점검에 불응했고, 결국 부분적인 점검만 이루어졌다. 일부만 점검했는데 이런 결과가 나온 것이다. 그리고 외부세력의 해킹의 위험성이 해소되지 않은 상태에서 2024년 총선이 치루어졌고 다수당이 된 민주당은 온갖 줄탄핵과 탄핵협박으로 윤석열 정부를 무력화시키고 붕괴로 몰아가는 내란을 일으켰다.  선관위는 선거전산망의 문제점을 지적해도 헌법기관이라며 모르쇠로 일관했다. 윤석열 대통령은 "지난 24년 4월 총선을 앞두고도 문제 있는 부분에 대한 개선을 요구했지만, 제대로 개선되었는지는 알 수 없습니다."라며 한탄했다. 선거 무결성에 대한 신뢰가 흔들리는 증거들은 많았지만 헌법기관이라는 이유로 선관위는 압수수색, 강제수사가 불가능했다. 국헌과 국체를 지켜야 하는 대통령으로서, 선관위의 이런 문제를 외면하는 것이 과연 옳은 것일까? 국민의 소중한 한표가 시스템 상에서 정확하게 집계되고 있다는 믿음이 깨져버린 상황이라면 이를 개선하고 필요한 조치들을 해야 한다. 헌법기관이라는 이유로 선관위가 이를 피하고 거대야당인 민주당이 선관위의 보호자처럼 행동하는 상황이라면 대통령은 비상대권을 동원해서라도 이를 바로 잡아야 한다. 이것이 윤석열 대통령이 계엄을 단행한 이유다.  트럼프 대통령과 윤석열 대통령은 동일한 문제의식을 가졌다. 선거의 무결성에 대한 의속이 그것이다. 이를 해결하려는 방법은 달랐다. 트럼프 대통령은 공론화의 방법을 선택했다. 윤석열 대통령은 비상대권 사용을 선택했다. 이는 두 사람이 처한 정치적 환경의 차이 때문이다. 한국의 민주당은 미국의 민주당 보다 더 악랄하게 윤석열 정부를 흔들고 있었다. 그리고 선거에 개입한 외부세력은 북한, 중국등 상당히 강력한 존재들이었다. 좌파언론에 세뇌당한 국민들은 윤석열 정부에 등을 돌리고 있었다. 윤 대통령에게는 선택의 길이 없었다.  그러나 두 사람은 모두 민주주의의 근간인 선거의 무결성이 흔들리고 있고 외세의 간섭과 부정이 자행되고 있음을 공론화 했다. 좌파들로부터 음모론자라고 공격받고 있는 것도 동일하다. 그러나 음모론으로 치부하기에는 증거가 너무 많다. 사람들은 조금씩 깨어나고 있다.  어쩌면 라그나로크 급 마지막 결전의 순간이 다가오고 있는 건지도 모른다.

만약 대만과 국교를 유지하고 CCP 중국공산당과는 낮은 레벨의 외교 관계를 맺을 수 있었는 지  앞으로 중공과 국교 단절을 하면 어떤 일이 발생하는 지 특히 off line 해야 해킹 피싱 등으로 인한 피해 방지하고 기술 유출 막으며 등 (희토류?)  노태우 정부 당시 북한은 소련이 붕괴하여 고난의 행군 입구였는데 어떤 세력이 작용하여 결정하였는 지는 알 길이 없고  CCP 는 밥먹고 살 만해 지니까 초한전으로 한국을 홍콩 처럼 먹자고 하다가   멸망의 문을 열었다 (부정선거 초토화 대상국가)  미국 법 체계로는 당장 북경 중남해를 B2 로 폐허 만들어도 되는 상황 (부정선거로 미국을 침공)  첨언: 92년 말 부터 중국공산당 간부들 만나보았다 베이징 상해 심천 청도 광동 많이 다녀봄  그당시 이 나라는 세월이 흘러도 강대국은 될 수 없다 라고 생각했다 (G2? 무슨 멍멍)  이유는? 인간의 기본권을 통제하는데 무슨 창의성이 나오며 무슨 애국심이 있을까 (세뇌되었을 뿐)  China 를 지배하는 사상은 무엇인가 라는 질문에 사석에서는 모택동이 아니고 '돈' 이라 답한다 거의 다   앞으로 대만에는 공식적으로 단교 사과해야 한다고 생각한다 그당시 갑자기 이별 통보  엄청난 반발이 있었다   참고: 한국 기술 도둑, 보이스피싱, 마약, 코로나 바이러스 그리고 그간 부정선거로 침공한 댓가로 만주국 반환에  많은 것을 요구하고 받아내야

(+본인 즐거움 추가)  (외모를 걸고 넘어지는 게 아니라, 이제 초등학교 갓 졸업한 나이에 돌려깎기부터 성형을 싸악 다 했더라고요. 정상적인 부모는 그 나이에 절대 자기 아이 뼈에 칼을 대지 않습니다. 자라는 나이에 큰 일. 날 수 있거든요. 쌍커플정도라면 모를까. ) -> “뭐하러요”, “ 요고조고” 인터뷰  (무삭제본 )  -> 가동차게 달리기 -> 갈색머리로 염색한 인터뷰에서 한 김 빠짐 (소원이룸) -> 디아블로 (2024. 아들이 아닌 다른 어린애 달래는 목소리 ) -> 제 2차 본인이 안 보는 날에도 집요한 요고조고. -> 오디오하이재킹으로 이미 바수어진 머리를 몰며 AI 타령과 제주도 집단정신병 (SCJ 총파티) 완성. -> 일론머스크 easy~ 자랑 ( SCJ 인터넷 접수하고 본격적으로 일론 머스크 인터넷으로 몰아 띄우기 가동 시작) SCJ (신천지) 와 함께 영성사기 동시 진행 -> 큰집으로 이사해 SCJ 둘러싸고 살기 (이탈리아 야외인터뷰 시절) -> 성범죄 공유 (’Ai는 진실만 말해야해서 … “길들여야죠 (변태표정)” 오디오하이재킹과 자신의 친구들을 이용한 정신해리 원격 강간시스템 구축 -> 성폭력 , 몰카, 프라이버시 침해, 집단스토킹을 일론 머스크가 한창 트위터를 이용해 찬양 SCJ컨텐츠와 머스크 똘똘 뭉쳐 대선 사기 (내 인생을 망친 잡것들 파느라 계속 보러다니는 나를 계속 이용해먹고 SCJ의 방식과 응원을 받아) -> “그들을 인생에 들여놨으니 받아들여야한다고 생각했어요”운운 > 대선사기 성공 (2024.12 파티댄스) -> 22-3년 당시 15세 정도였던 신천지 미성년자 매춘부와 내 계정와서 깽판치고 지들끼리 축하붕가. (중간에 나와 조롱) -> 아침, ‘멋진 자신 (상처받아버렸고 보여준 나) 새사진‘ 찍어 돌리고 매춘부 화장실 던지고 SCJ들 일제히 물어뜯기 -> 많이 자란 애와 ’이제 사랑을 키워가는 상황’ 운운하며 본격 신접살림 (?) ->  SCJ 급증. 개인정보 빼돌림 (SCJ포교용) -> 2025.4 원격강간시스템 자랑하며 ‘Good Sound🎶, isn’t it?’ 타령하며 아주 신이 남. -> 2025.5 한 대 맞고 사임 (정신흔들림) -> 이후 SCJ(신천지) 와 함께 열심히 나를 성적으로 몰아부치며 원격강간시스템 이용하여 내가 여행가거나 무너질때마다 인터뷰 땀 -> 본격 (SCJ) god 타령 (영상사기 지속으로 이미지 만화용) 처맞고 내려온 기억을 나로 덮어 이미지 덮는데 성공. (저는 여전히 뇌살인에 시달리고 있었어요, 페낭까지 그랬죠. 💁🏻 얘네 계획에는 SCJ 오디오하이재킹도 들어가 있았죠, 애초에. 뭐라 뒤에서 처발랐든.)  -> 나를 죽어라 물고 뜯으며 세력확장하는 SCJ (신천지) -> 나 발리갔다가 나오란 소리듣고 한국 탈출, -> 머스크 이때부터 본격적으로 SCJ와 개썩은 짓거리하고 다니며 개썩어서 얼굴 시커매져선 금요일만 지나면 세 마디 이상의 단어를 못하게 됨 (이후 처바름) -> SCJ 내가 예약한 호텔마다 일본 ~ 홍콩까지 처박기 (도쿄 고급호텔 직원이 업무내용을 잘 모름) -> 해킹과 집단스토킹을 이용해 이른바 SCJ와 ’갖고놀기‘ (SCJ 비행기 단위로 집단스토킹 이동) -> 교토의 집단스토킹으로 인한 부상 (정신이 없어 넘어져 머리를 돌에 찧어 반신불구 정도는 될 뻔 했으나 하느님께서 지켜주시어 어깨부상으로 끝남 -> 이후 어개부상 시킨 것 자랑 -> 처음 가보는 동네에서 핸드폰 구글맵 해킹하여 사람을 무거운 캐리어 두 개 끌고 이틀동안 먹지도 씻지도 쉬지도 못하게 하여 뺑글뺑글 돌리며 SCJ(신천지)의 ’갖고놀기. ’ 그날이 쟤 SCJ 길들여진 매춘부 베이비와 섹스하는 그쪽 금요일, 그 시간대 ) -> 이후 계속 같은 상황이 반복되고 지도관 SCJ의 집단스토킹에 시달리며 개고생하며 일본 렌트키 여행 -> 히로시마에선 사지에 몰아넣고, (지도안내 해킹) 그때도 쟤가 SCJ에서 길들여진 매춘부와 붕가하는 그쪽 금요일, 그 시간대.가는 호텔마다 SCJ 호텔단의 점령 -> 홍콩에서도 이어지는 집단스토킹과 호텔단위 점령. 계속 됨 협박과 괴상한 짓거리들, 쉴틈없는 스토킹 -> 홍콩공항에서 얘 SCj가 길들이고 원판 고친 섹스파크나 매춘부 마스크끼고 등장 -> 이후 SCJ 지들끼리 파티. (지하를 점령해 빨래 못 돌려서 암) -> 일론머스크 영상 SCJ에 대대적으로 돌림 -> SCJ 성적모욕 퍼부으며 개신나고 분위기 열심히 조장하고 다님 -> 머스크 SCJ모아놓고 자기가 한 짓거리 자랑하며 신남-> 말레이시아 페낭 도착. -> 그 건물에 SCJㄹ 계속 번갈아 들어옴 +집단스토킹, 가택칩입과 성착취 (강간) 시도, 머스크가 위성 띄워줌 (몰카설치 실패로) -> 강간시도한 한국 왕따놈 (스토커이자 몰카설치배포 성범죄자, 머스크의 베프 중 하나) 나오라고 했다가 경찰오고 머스크가 시커먼 얼굴로 “Empty ㅎㅎ” 타령. ->

<“한국은 안전한가?”: 미국 대선 폭로가 한국 선거 제도에 던지는 경고장> 미국 정보당국의 기밀 해제 문건과 트럼프 대통령의 연설이 전 세계를 뒤흔들고 있다. 적성국의 사이버 개입, 선거 장비의 보안 취약성, 내부 관료들의 은폐 정황이 공식 안보 문서로 입증된 것이다. 그렇다면 우리는 이 질문을 던져야 한다. "지리적·기술적으로 중국의 직접적인 영향력 아래 놓인 대한민국은 과연 이 위협으로부터 안전한가?"  한국에서 제기되는 '외세 개입 및 선거 부정 의혹'을 결코 허무맹랑한 음모론으로 치부할 수 없는 세 가지 실체적 이유가 있다.  1. 선관위의 처참하고 부패한 선거관리와 무너진 신뢰 장벽 선거의 생명은 '티끌만 한 의혹도 허용하지 않는 철저함'에 있다. 하지만 지난 6·3 지방선거에서 보여준 선관위의 행태는 처참했다. 헌법기관의 선거 관리라고는 믿기 힘든 수준의 방만경영과 부패의혹, 행정적 구멍은 국민적 불신을 자초했다. "미국보다 선거 제도의 구멍이 더 많고 허술하다"는 세간의 비판이 뼈아프게 와닿는 대목이다.  2. ‘미루·한틀’과 안보 당국이 공식 경고한 기술적 취약점 미루시스템즈와 한틀시스템이 납품 생태계를 양분해 온 국내 투·개표 시스템의 실상은 충격적이다.  대한민국 국가정보원(NIS)과 한국인터넷진흥원(KISA)의 합동 보안 점검에서 드러난 핵심 사실은 다음과 같다. 투·개표 조작 가능성 공식 확인모의 해킹 결과, 해커가 선관위 내부망에 침입해 유권자 명부를 조작하거나 사전투표자를 미투표자로 바꾸는 것이 가능했다. 심지어 개표 과정에서 투표지 분류기의 전산 결과값을 임의로 변경하는 침투 시나리오까지 성공했다. 보안 점수 조작과 조직적 은폐선관위는 원래 80점대였던 자체 보안 점수를 국정원에 '100점 만점'으로 허위 보고했으나, 실제 점검 결과는 31.5점에 불과했다. 국정원의 전산 점검 도구를 선관위 직원이 무단 삭제하고, 북한 소행 해킹 사고를 은폐하려 한 정황도 적발됐다. 미국 CISA가 경고했던 소프트웨어 공급망 및 내부 관리 부실의 위험이, 한국에서는 미루·한틀시스템 장비와 이를 감독해야 할 선관위의 무능·허위 보고를 통해 그대로 현실화된 셈이다. 국가 근간을 흔드는 보안 구멍이 드러난 만큼, 이제는 정파를 초월한 투명한 감사가 즉각 실행되어야 한다.  3. A-WEB의 '글로벌 선거 개입' 의혹과 부작용 한국 중앙선관위의 주도로 설립된 세계선거기관협의회(A-WEB)를 둘러싼 국제적 의혹은 이 논쟁의 무대를 세계로 넓힌다. A-WEB이 공적개발원조(ODA)를 통해 콩고민주공화국이나 필리핀 등 선거가 불투명한 제3세계 국가들에 한국산 전자 개표 장비(미루시스템즈 등)를 수출하는 과정에서, 현지 야당과 시민단체들로부터 "한국이 불완전하고 조작이 용이한 기계 선거를 이식해 독재 정권을 연장시켰다"는 거센 비판과 시위를 유발했다. 글로벌 선거 장비 수출 카르텔의 온상이 바로 우리 손으로 만든 기구라는 혐의는 대단히 무거운 외교·안보적 리스크다.  결과적으로, 중국공산당의 보이지 않는 하이브리드 전쟁과 사이버 영향력이 날로 노골화되는 대한민국이다. 이런 상황에서 ① 공정성, 투명성은 1도 찾아보기 어려운 선관위의 관리, ② 공식 입증된 전산망의 보안 구멍, ③ 국제적으로 시비를 낳은 선거 장비 수출 이력까지 겹쳐 있다.  이 모든 정황을 보고도 "한국의 부정선거 의혹은 그저 음모론일 뿐"이라고 비웃는 것이야말로 진정한 '안보 불감증'이다.  미국이 최고 정보기관들을 총동원해 대대적인 수사에 착수한 것처럼, 대한민국 역시 국가의 근간을 흔드는 선거 보안 위협에 대해 정파를 초월한 전면적이고 투명한 감사를 즉각 실시해야 한다.  #부정선거 #중국개입 #미국 #대한민국 #AWEB #전자개표기 #중앙선거관리위원회

The Fourth Power Doctrine: France Completes the Eastern Mediterranean Centre. Poseidon’s Wrath Enters Ankara’s War Calculus  👇 Line of State Strategic Doctrine | @rieasgreece, Athens  The Eastern Mediterranean needs a centre, not a patron.  Israel, Greece and Cyprus are it. France completes it.  Ankara systematises coercion. Geography supplies leverage; NATO membership, insulation; occupation, depth; migration, pressure; defence exports, reach. Turkey fragments the region into bilateral disputes, isolates each capital and enters every negotiation from the ground it has already seized.  The United States remains indispensable in a wider war but cannot anchor the regional order. Washington recognises the Turkish threat while preserving Ankara’s utility in NATO, the Black Sea, Syria, migration and regional diplomacy. Paris cannot make that trade at Greek and Cypriot expense without forfeiting its Mediterranean position.  The Fourth Power Doctrine operationalises Lisbon 2.0 in the Eastern Mediterranean. Article 42(7) must become force, industry, stockpiles, mobility, command and reflex.  Turkey can veto NATO. It cannot veto Lisbon.  France alone combines presidential command, an independent nuclear deterrent, sovereign intelligence, carrier aviation, expeditionary reach, industrial autonomy and a permanent Security Council seat. French power deters attack, sustains the campaign and insulates victory from lawfare and diplomatic reversal.  Paris has converted alignment into armed commitment. The 2021 treaty binds French credibility to Greek defence. Access, exercises, deployments and the strategic partnership have embedded Cyprus in French operational geography. Article 42(7) requires neither a NATO decision nor Turkish consent.  The Constantinople Protocol addresses coercion before war. Turkish power begins not with closure but administration: a port order, an overflight restriction, a delayed service, an insurance condition or regulatory ambiguity. Administration breeds discretion. Discretion hardens into veto.  The answer is redundancy. Ports, airspace, energy corridors, cables and logistics must operate independently of Turkish consent. No Turkish gate can remain singular.  Paris and Jerusalem read Cyprus, Syria, Libya, Hamas, maritime pressure and access routes as parts of one Turkish architecture. Political disputes have not altered that map. The two capitals need no intimacy, only a protected Turkey channel across defence, diplomacy, security and intelligence.  The Palestinian file divides them. Ankara compels convergence. France requires institutions, demilitarisation, a functioning Palestinian Authority and a negotiated settlement. Turkey legitimises Hamas, hollows out the Authority and weaponises Palestinian suffering.  Turkey supports Palestine in language and destroys its statehood in practice.  SAMP/T will decide whether France remains the exception.  A guarantee that activates only after attack is law without strategy. Export law is collective defence before the first strike.  Turkey occupies northern Cyprus, retains a casus belli against Greece, militarises the occupation and contests the sovereignty of two European Union members. SAMP/T must remain closed to Turkey in every form: export, transfer, sustainment, certification, integration and joint production.  France cannot shield the state threatening the allies it is bound to defend.  Poseidon’s Wrath begins when Turkey weaponises occupied Cypriot territory against the Republic of Cyprus, Greece, Israel or critical infrastructure. The occupation becomes both weapon and theatre.  The war aim is restoration: defeat the attack, block Turkish reinforcement, dismantle the occupation’s military infrastructure, remove Turkish control and restore the Republic of Cyprus across the island.  Not contain it. Not refreeze it. End it.  The Security Council has declared the attempted secession invalid. Turkish Cypriots remain equal citizens of the Republic. Settlers brought after 1974 without lawful status are repatriated to Turkey. Sovereignty, security, property and civil order return to Nicosia.  Nicosia sets the trigger and end state. Athens provides bases, continuity and the nearest Hellenic force. Jerusalem delivers intelligence, early warning, air and missile defence, cyber, precision and the tempo that breaks the opening phase. Paris begins where the strike ends: it holds the maritime theatre, seals reinforcement routes, protects the seabed and critical corridors, manages escalation and consolidates victory.  Scenarios matching Poseidon’s Wrath already sit on French defence planning tables: the defence of Cyprus, rapid deployment, combined action with Greece, allied reinforcement and sustained access from the Levant through Suez to the Red Sea.  Paris has moved beyond prevention and containment. It is preparing to enter the theatre in force, sustain the campaign and turn victory into durable order.  Turkey makes the war French by attacking European territory, a French ally or the Mediterranean order on which French power rests.  The four states must establish a permanent classified architecture aligning threat assessment, attribution, missions, communications, logistics and the political end state.  France does not command the centre.  It makes it irreversible.  At the hour of decision, France must be there.  🔗 rieas.gr/researchareas/edito…

CCDCOE just published the technical and legal anatomy of something that has been happening continuously since the 1980s, which is itself a kind of finding.  Scenario 02 in their international cyber law toolkit documents the standard playbook for state-sponsored government network espionage: initial access, privilege escalation, lateral movement through infrastructure that spans multiple national jurisdictions. The cross-border server piece isn't incidental. It's structural. Adversaries route intrusions through servers in third countries specifically because the legal questions that follow, who has standing, which law applies, how attribution gets established, tend to outlast the political will to answer them.  The trajectory here is not complicated, but it is underappreciated.  The underlying strategic logic, penetrating adversary government communications, has been constant since at least the early Cold War. What changed was the attack surface. Governments digitized their operations, which means the same intelligence targets that previously required a recruited asset inside a ministry or a signals intercept of a diplomatic cable are now reachable remotely, persistently, and across borders. Salt Typhoon in the 2020s is the same strategic objective as the Soviet-adjacent operations of 1981. The methods are different. The ambition is identical.  So what does that trajectory actually suggest about where this is heading?  First, the jurisdictional complexity will get worse before any legal framework catches up to it. The CCDCOE scenario documents cross-border server exploitation as a core feature of these operations, not an occasional complication. State actors have had forty years of practice routing intrusions through legally ambiguous infrastructure, and they are not going to stop because international lawyers are writing better scenarios. The gap between the pace of operations and the pace of legal development is large and is not closing. Any government that is waiting for a clear international legal prohibition on peacetime cyber espionage to materialize before deciding how to respond is going to be waiting for a long time.  Second, the privilege escalation and lateral movement signatures that CCDCOE documents so carefully are useful for attribution after the fact. They are less useful for detection during the operation, which is when it matters. The Salt Typhoon intrusions into U.S. telecommunications infrastructure persisted long enough to give the attackers access to lawful intercept systems. By the time attribution is solid enough to support a diplomatic expulsion or a DOJ indictment, the access has already been used. Defenders watching this pattern should be thinking about detection windows, not just forensic signatures.  Third, the legal accountability mechanisms that democratic governments have developed, sanctions, indictments, coordinated diplomatic expulsions, have not demonstrably deterred persistent government network infiltration. They are important for signaling, norm development, and domestic political accountability. They have not shortened the duration of intrusions or reduced their frequency. This is a real tension that the CCDCOE framework documents without fully resolving, which is honest of them.  What officials should actually be doing with this, rather than treating the CCDCOE toolkit as an academic exercise: the scenario's identification of cross-border server exploitation as structurally embedded in these operations should be driving conversations about mutual legal assistance treaties, about which third-country relationships matter most for rapid coordination when an intrusion path runs through their infrastructure, and about whether the current response timeline, which can take years from intrusion to public attribution, is compatible with protecting the systems being targeted.  Voters and journalists are unlikely to find the privilege escalation technical detail gripping. But the cross-border jurisdictional piece is actually accessible and important: when a foreign intelligence service uses servers in a neutral country to access a government network, the targeted government often cannot legally compel that neutral country to hand over logs, and the attacker knows this at the time of planning. It is not a bug in their approach. It is the approach.  The CCDCOE doing this work carefully and publicly is genuinely valuable. The concern is that the gap it documents, between what state actors can do technically and what international law can address legally, has been documented before, including in the Yale Journal of Law and Technology analysis and the CFR Cyber Operations Tracker, without producing a corresponding reduction in the operations being documented. Naming the gap is necessary. It is not sufficient.  The next intrusion into a government network that spans multiple jurisdictions is not a future event being planned. It is an ongoing event that has not been discovered yet.  foreigninterference.org/post… #foreigninterference #AdvancedPersistentThreatOperations #CyberEspionage #PersistentNetworkInfiltration #LivingOffTheLandExploitation #InternationalLawGapExploitation

Cyber police have arrested a 26‑year‑old man in downtown Phuket who, despite having only a primary school education, taught himself advanced hacking techniques using AI chatbots.   Investigators revealed that the suspect initially attempted to breach police systems to erase his own arrest warrants before turning his attention to private websites with weaker defenses.  The Cyber Police told the Phuket Express that the suspect, identified only as Sahachat, admitted during questioning that he once tried to infiltrate the Royal Thai Police database to remove his criminal record.   When that failed, he shifted to exploiting vulnerabilities in commercial platforms.   His most recent target was Firstup.in.th, a website offering online game top‑ups and mobile credit services.  By manipulating the payment gateway, he tricked the system into believing transactions were successful, generating fake credits that he later exchanged for prepaid game codes.   The company reported losses of more than 200,000 baht.  Police Cyber Taskforce Commander Pol. Lt. Gen. Surapol Prembutr ordered a joint operation with Region 8 officers. Armed with search and arrest warrants, they raided a rented room in Phuket’s Talad Yai district.   Officers seized desktop computers, laptops, four mobile phones, a firearm with ammunition, and 80 methamphetamine pills.  Investigators also discovered that Sahachat was already wanted under five separate warrants for robbery, fraud, and computer crime offenses.  During interrogation, the suspect confessed that while hiding near the Myanmar border, he relied on AI chatbots and other AI tools to study coding, analyze system vulnerabilities, and build attack programs.   He described AI as his “teacher,” guiding him through technical concepts he had never formally studied.  Cybersecurity experts warn that this case shows how AI can accelerate the learning curve for malicious actors. Without regular system updates and strong defenses, organizations risk becoming easy targets for AI‑assisted attacks.  Authorities have filed several serious charges against Sahachat, including:  Theft and receiving stolen property Unauthorized access to computer systems and data Altering or destroying computer information Causing disruption to computer systems Possession of methamphetamine with intent to sell and drug use Illegal possession of firearms and ammunition  thephuketexpress.com/2026/07…

sample_tweets.text: A hacker stole over $220,000 in crypto by hiding malware inside Steam games:  - Some reports claim an arrest has been made.  - At the time of writing, the FBI and DOJ have not independently confirmed those reports.  Here's what we do know.  - Earlier this year, several games on Steam were found hiding malware instead of just game files.  - They looked completely normal.  - People downloaded and played them without realising anything was wrong.  - But in the background, the malware was:  > browser data > stealing passwords > saved login credentials > and crypto wallet information.  The affected games reportedly included:  - Lampy - PirateFi - Dashverse - and Lunara - BlockBlasters  - Around 8,000 computers were reportedly infected.  - More than 80 crypto wallets were compromised.  - Over $220,000 worth of crypto was stolen.  - Valve removed the games from Steam and urged affected users to scan their systems and change all passwords.  - The FBI has been investigating the campaign for months.  - Now, some reports claim a 21 year old Florida student has been arrested in connection with the operation.  - However, there has been no official FBI or DOJ announcement confirming those claims at the time of writing.  - We'll update the story if official charges or court documents are released.

Unit42 found a malware sample that looked exactly like Equation Group.  same exported function names, API usage patterns. same third-party driver abuse for kernel access. multiple vendors had already auto-classified it as a new EquationDrug variant.  Equation Group is widely attributed to the NSA. one of the most sophisticated threat actors ever documented. if this was them, it was significant.  then someone checked the VirusTotal submission metadata.  the sample had been uploaded from Oxford, Mississippi.  not a known NSA location. not a government IP range. Oxford, Mississippi.  they pulled that thread and concluded it almost certainly wasn't Equation Group at all.  which means one of two things happened:  someone reverse-engineered Equation Group's style so precisely, the function names, the API calls, the driver abuse patterns — that automated classification systems and experienced analysts both got fooled.  or the original attribution of those "Equation Group" patterns was wrong from the start. and everything built on top of that attribution has the same flaw.  this is what makes malware attribution one of the hardest problems in cybersecurity.  style can be copied. patterns can be replicated. a threat actor who studies known malware families long enough can build something that looks like someone else.  called false flag operations. documented. deliberate. effective.  the Oxford sample was either a sophisticated impersonation or proof that the baseline was wrong.  either answer is uncomfortable.  because the entire intelligence community uses these patterns to attribute attacks to nation-states.  and attribute nation-states to geopolitical decisions.  an analyst in Oxford, Mississippi just broke that chain.

This malware marks a distinct evolution from opportunistic worm behavior toward an industrial-grade, ROI-driven attack platform aimed squarely at Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) infrastructure.  By outsourcing initial reconnaissance tasks to Shodan rather than relying solely on slow, resource-heavy brute-force internet scanning, NadMesh’s operators can immediately zero in on live AI deployments instead of wasting bandwidth on dead address space.  Unlike traditional worms that spread indiscriminately, NadMesh combines autonomous scanning, over 20 unique exploitation vectors, and Shodan-powered intelligence harvesting into a single closed-loop system its operator refers to as the “n4d mesh controller”.  The most distinctive feature of NadMesh is a dedicated reconnaissance module named ai_harvest.py.

🚨 Threat Intelligence | Fake Recruitment Campaign Delivers Malware via GitHub Repository  MistEye recently detected a malicious campaign targeting developers through fake Web3 recruitment. Attackers impersonated recruiters on LinkedIn, built trust through discussions about work experience and interviews, then sent a GitHub repository disguised as an “interview MVP” and tricked developers into running the project.  Our analysis found that the malicious repository hid theme/js/auron-core.min.js as a Tailwind plugin. When developers ran the project’s development or build commands, the hidden Node.js loader was triggered and deployed multiple payloads for:  🔹 Browser credential & wallet data theft 🔹 Sensitive file collection and exfiltration 🔹 Remote command execution and interactive Shell access 🔹 Clipboard monitoring  This campaign shows how trusted development workflows can become attack vectors. Developers should always inspect project scripts, dependencies, and build configurations before running unknown repositories.  Full analysis👇 slowmist.medium.com/threat-i…
